pattern: A day-indexed dynamic program chooses, at each travel day, the cheapest pass that covers it by looking back over the windows the three pass lengths span.
hints: Think about the minimum cost to cover all travel up to and including each day.
On a travel day you either bought a 1-, 7-, or 30-day pass that ends here; each choice points back to an earlier already-solved day.

Let dp[d] be the min cost to cover every travel day up to d. On a travel day take min(dp[d-1]+costs[0], dp[d-7]+costs[1], dp[d-30]+costs[2]) clamping indices below 1 to 0; on a non-travel day dp[d]=dp[d-1].

edge_inputs.description: single travel day uses the cheapest single pass
clustered days favor a weekly pass
ten consecutive days plus a far day favor the monthly pass
when daily pass is cheapest no bundling helps
monthly pass cheapest even for few days
last possible day of the year

stress.description: random strictly-increasing travel days across the year
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 365))
    days = sorted(rng.sample(range(1, 366), n))
    costs = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(3)]
    return [days, costs]
stress.note: rng.sample guarantees strictly increasing distinct days
